response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad experience across web, mobile, and distributed systems, but his profile does not align with the core requirements of this role. His C++ experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2007–2008 and a bachelor-thesis project; his primary languages for the past 15+ years have been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443, SDL), no familiarity with industrial communication protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), and no IoT or automation-industry background. While his event-driven architecture and distributed-systems work is transferable, the gaps in the mandatory technical competencies are too significant for a mid-senior C++ role in industrial automation.
strengths: Demonstrated event-driven and distributed-systems architecture experience at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O) and GISCollective
Strong agile and software-craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development applied consistently across roles
Broad polyglot background with some systems-level exposure (DLang, early C/C++, embedded work at Altom with robotic arms)
concerns: C++ is not an active skill — last meaningful use was 2007–2008 at National Instruments; no STL, multi-threading, or modern C++ evidence
Zero evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge: no IEC 62443, no SDL, no mention of secure coding in embedded or OT contexts
No industrial protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850) and no IoT or automation-industry domain background
